sheet.label: API DESIGN
sheet.title: 7. API Design & Communication Flow
sheet.p: The Theta Chat Bot follows a RESTful API architecture, where the chatbot widget communicates with the FastAPI backend through secure HTTPS endpoints.

The backend acts as the central orchestration layer responsible for validating requests, retrieving relevant knowledge, communicating with the Large Language Model (LLM), and returning structured responses to the frontend.
All communication between components is performed using JSON payloads, making the APIs lightweight, language-independent, and easy to integrate with existing or future frontend applications.
The communication between system components follows the sequence below:
Each component has a clearly defined responsibility, ensuring loose coupling and easier maintenance.
The following endpoints are proposed for the initial version of the chatbot.
Process user queries and generate chatbot responses
Create or resume a user session
Submit user feedback on chatbot responses
Health check for monitoring and deployment validation
Retrieve chatbot configuration and feature flags
Future administrative endpoints may include analytics, knowledge synchronization, and dashboard APIs.
When a visitor submits a message, the frontend sends a POST request to the chatbot API.
"message": "What AI services does Theta Technolabs provide?",
Unique identifier for the current browser session
Upon receiving the request, the backend performs the following operations:
Retrieve the Top-K relevant content chunks.
Send the prompt to the configured LLM.
Return the final response to the frontend.
Each step is executed sequentially to ensure consistent and reliable responses.
The backend returns a standardized JSON response.
"response": "Theta Technolabs provides AI Development, Machine Learning, Computer Vision, and Conversational AI solutions.",
Website pages used to generate the response
Confidence level assigned to the response
Including source references increases transparency and allows users to verify the information directly on the official website.
The API returns standardized error responses whenever a request cannot be completed successfully.
"message": "Relevant information could not be found within the current knowledge base."
Providing standardized error codes simplifies debugging and future monitoring.
Although the chatbot is publicly accessible and does not require user authentication, temporary browser sessions are maintained to support conversational context.
Each visitor receives a unique session identifier when opening the chatbot.
No personally identifiable information (PII) is required to create or maintain a session.
To protect the chatbot APIs from misuse, the backend implements multiple validation and security mechanisms.
Secure server-side communication with LLM providers
Sensitive API credentials are never exposed to the frontend.
All public endpoints are versioned using URI-based versioning.
Versioning allows future API enhancements without breaking compatibility with existing frontend integrations.
Simple, widely supported, and easy to integrate.
Improves conversational continuity without requiring user authentication.
Supports future enhancements while maintaining backward compatibility.
